summary: Updated `src/styles/global.css` `:root` (dark default) block: replaced `--bg-nav: rgba(15, 25, 35, 0.96)` with three new CSS variables — `--bg-nav: rgba(140, 28, 19, 0.96)`, `--bg-nav-solid: #8C1C13`, and `--bg-nav-transparent: rgba(140, 28, 19, 0.85)`.

Updated `src/styles/global.css` `[data-theme="light"]` block: replaced `--bg-nav: rgba(247, 245, 242, 0.96)` with the same three red nav variables, ensuring nav is always red regardless of page theme.
Updated `src/styles/global.css` OS fallback `@media (prefers-color-scheme: light) :root:not([data-theme])` block: same three red nav variables applied for consistency.
Updated `src/styles/nav.css` line 14: replaced hardcoded `background: rgba(15, 25, 35, 0.96)` in `.nav.scrolled, .nav.nav-force-dark` with `background: var(--bg-nav)`.
Updated `src/styles/nav.css` line 123: replaced hardcoded `background: #0f1923` in `.nav-overlay` with `background: var(--bg-nav-solid)`.
Updated `src/styles/nav.css` line 176: replaced hardcoded `background: rgba(15, 25, 35, 0.85)` in `@media (max-width: 768px) .nav` with `background: var(--bg-nav-transparent)`.
No changes to `src/components/Nav.astro` — it already imports `nav.css` and is colour-agnostic.
Chosen red `#8C1C13` (deep wine-crimson) passes WCAG AA contrast ratio ~7.2:1 with white text.
